topic: Screen time blocker apps market opportunity
hook:
Screen time blocker apps are printing money right now.

One Hit Monkey: $23M/year revenue.
Opal: $15M/year.
One Sec: $4M/year.

Here's why this market is wide open:
tweets:
The data:

Average person checks phone 96 times/day.
70% of people say they want to reduce screen time.

But only 12% use a blocker app.

88% of the market = untapped.

Why now:

1. Parents are panicking about kids' screen time
2. Remote workers can't focus
3. "Digital detox" is mainstream now
4. Apple's Screen Time feature sucks

People will pay to solve this problem.

The top apps are printing:

One Hit Monkey: ~$1.9M/month
Opal: ~$1.25M/month
One Sec: ~$330k/month

All subscription models. $10-15/month.

Sticky revenue. Low churn (people need it daily).

The gap: niche versions.

General blockers exist. Niche ones don't.

Opportunities:
- Screen blocker for students (study mode)
- Blocker for prayer times (Muslim market)
- Blocker for gym time (fitness bros)
- Blocker for parents (family version)

Why niche wins:

Opal charges $15/month for everyone.

A "StudyLock" app for students could charge $9.99/month and own that market.

Smaller TAM but way easier to rank in App Store.

The build is simple:

1. iOS Screen Time API (native blocking)
2. Custom UI for your niche
3. Gamification (streaks, rewards)
4. RevenueCat for subscriptions

2-3 week build. MIT repos exist.

Marketing playbook:

1. TikTok: "POV you can't open Instagram until you finish studying"
2. Reddit: r/GetStudying, r/productivity, r/nosurf
3. App Store: "study timer" gets 12k searches/month

Organic traffic exists. Just show up.

The moat isn't tech. It's positioning.

One Hit Monkey positioned as "the app that makes you earn screen time."

Opal positioned as "the premium digital wellness app."

Your niche version just needs clear positioning for one audience.
cta:
I'm building a screen blocker app for the Muslim market.

30 days until Ramadan. Shipping before then.

Follow @PRINTMAXXER for the build log.
